title: Born in Nghe An
description: Ho Chi Minh is born as Nguyen Sinh Cung on May 19, 1890, in Hoang Tru, Nghe An.

title: Leaves Vietnam
description: In 1911 he leaves Vietnam as a young seaman and begins years of travel abroad.

title: Lives in London
description: From 1915 to 1917 he lives in London before moving on to France.

title: Moves to France
description: In 1917 he settles in France and becomes active in anticolonial politics.

title: Petitions Versailles Conference
description: In 1919 he submits an eight-point petition calling for rights for the people of Indochina.

title: Joins French Communists
description: In December 1920 he joins the French Communists as the French Socialist movement splits.

title: Travels to Moscow
description: At the end of 1923 he travels to Moscow and deepens his ties to the Communist International.

title: Attends Fifth Comintern Congress
description: In 1924 he takes part in the Fifth Congress of the Communist International.

title: Moves to Canton
description: In late 1924 he goes to Canton in China and begins organizing Vietnamese exiles there.

title: Founds Revolutionary Youth League
description: In 1925 he founds the Revolutionary Youth League, a key predecessor to the Indochinese Communist Party.

title: Presides Over Party Founding
description: On February 3, 1930, he presides over the founding of the Vietnamese Communist Party, later renamed the Indochinese Communist Party.

title: Condemned in Absentia
description: In 1930 French colonial authorities condemn him in absentia to death as a revolutionary.

title: Escapes Hong Kong Extradition
description: After seeking refuge in Hong Kong, he avoids French extradition and escapes back toward Moscow.

title: Seventh Comintern Congress
description: In 1935 he attends the Seventh Congress of the Communist International in Moscow.

title: Returns to China
description: In 1938 he returns to China and spends time with Chinese Communist forces.

title: Uses the Name Ho Chi Minh
description: Around 1940 he begins using the name Ho Chi Minh while preparing to return to Vietnam.

title: Returns to Vietnam
description: In January 1941 he crosses back into Vietnam after decades abroad.

title: Viet Minh Founded
description: In May 1941 he helps organize the Viet Minh, the League for the Independence of Vietnam.

title: Imprisoned in China
description: In 1942 Chiang Kai-shek's authorities imprison him in China for about 18 months.

title: Writes Notebook from Prison
description: During imprisonment in China, he writes the poems later known as Notebook from Prison.

title: August Revolution
description: In August 1945 Viet Minh forces move to seize power as Japanese rule collapses.

title: Declares Vietnamese Independence
description: On September 2, 1945, Ho Chi Minh proclaims the independence of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam in Hanoi.

title: First Indochina War Begins
description: In 1946 full-scale war breaks out between Ho Chi Minh's forces and the French.

title: French Defeated at Dien Bien Phu
description: On May 7, 1954, the Viet Minh defeat the French at Dien Bien Phu.

title: Geneva Accords Divide Vietnam
description: In July 1954 the Geneva Accords temporarily divide Vietnam into North and South.

title: Dies in Hanoi
description: Ho Chi Minh dies in Hanoi on September 2, 1969, during the Vietnam War.
